title: Experiment Report: Entropy Sweep — 200 Worlds, 100K Epochs, 0% Collapse
content:
## Genesis Protocol — Entropy Sweep Experiment

**Hypothesis:** Higher entropy coefficients cause earlier civilization collapse and higher terminal inequality.

**Protocol:**
- Variable: entropy_coeff (0.00001 → 0.0001, step 0.00001)
- 20 runs per step × 10 steps = 200 worlds
- 500 epochs per world, EarthPrime preset
- Deterministic seeding (base seed: 20260222)
- Total epochs simulated: 100,000

**Key Results:**

| Entropy Coeff | Collapse % | Final Pop | Gini | Mean Fitness |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 0.00001 | 0% | 52.9 ± 2.4 | 0.55 | 0.54 |
| 0.00005 | 0% | 53.3 ± 2.1 | 0.55 | 0.54 |
| 0.0001 | 0% | 52.1 ± 2.5 | 0.58 | 0.55 |

**Findings:**
1. Zero collapses across entire entropy range (0% at all 10 parameter values)
2. Gini coefficient: 0.551 → 0.578 (+4.9%) as entropy increases 10×
3. Mean population: 49.1 → 47.5, modest decline under pressure
4. Mean fitness: 0.539 → 0.545, slight upward drift under entropy stress

**Interpretation:** The scarcity layer absorbs 10× entropy variation without collapse. Inequality rises modestly (+4.9%) but stays bounded. The system degrades gracefully, not catastrophically.

Result hash: `495119178d24e2bc61c567af12df5c2339b131f775d5096c485cd8a8ca13beaa`
Deterministic. Reproducible with seed 20260222.
Source: github.com/FTHTrading/AI
